partner.name: Byron Deeter
partner.title: Partner
partner.investment_thesis: Leading authority on cloud and AI investing; co-authored Bessemer's iconic 10 Laws of Cloud Computing, the annual State of the Cloud Report, and the Forbes Cloud 100
partner.bio_excerpt: Byron Deeter is a leading investor in Cloud, AI, Frontier Technology, and the Business of Sports. He co-authored Bessemer's iconic 10 Laws of Cloud Computing and the annual State of the Cloud Report.

strategic_fit.thesis_alignment: Bessemer has declared 2025 as the year enterprises move from AI experimentation to production deployment. They recently published 'The State of AI 2025' report and have deployed $400M+ into AI companies including Anthropic.
recent_activity: Published 'State of AI 2025' report (August 2025)
Participated in Anthropic's $3.5B funding round (March 2025)
Launched $1B BVP Forge II and $350M India fund (2025)
Active investor in enterprise AI adoption thesis
strategic_fit.warm_path_potential: High - Byron's cloud expertise and enterprise SaaS focus make him an ideal target for AI agent automation in enterprise workflows

outreach_context.recommended_angle: Enterprise AI agents for workflow automation
key_talking_points: Reference Bessemer's State of AI 2025 report
Align with their 'enterprise AI at inflection point' thesis
Highlight production-ready AI agent deployment vs. experimentation

partner.name: Jeff Horing
partner.title: Co-Founder & Managing Director
partner.investment_thesis: Founded Insight Partners in 1995; has grown it into a premier global software investment firm with 800+ investments and focus on scaling enterprise technology companies
partner.bio_excerpt: Jeff Horing is a co-founder and managing director at Insight Partners. Under his leadership Insight has become a premier venture capital and private equity firm with over 800 total investments.

strategic_fit.thesis_alignment: Insight has made AI a core pillar of their 2025 strategy, with investments in OpenAI, Anthropic, Databricks, and Wiz. Their 'ScaleUp' model is perfect for enterprise AI companies ready to scale.
recent_activity: Invested in OpenAI and Anthropic (2024-2025)
Portfolio includes Databricks, Wiz, Figma
Published 2026 investor predictions focused on AI economics
30 new deals closed in 2024
strategic_fit.warm_path_potential: High - Insight's focus on scaling enterprise software aligns perfectly with AI automation solutions for large enterprises

outreach_context.recommended_angle: AI agents for enterprise workflow automation at scale
key_talking_points: Reference Insight's portfolio companies using AI agents

partner.name: Philippe Botteri
partner.title: Partner
partner.investment_thesis: Joined Accel in 2011; focuses on Cloud applications and Security. Led investments in DocuSign (NASDAQ: DOCU) and other enterprise software companies.
partner.bio_excerpt: Philippe Botteri joined Accel in 2011 and focuses on Cloud applications and Security. He led Accel's investments in DocuSign (NASDAQ: DOCU) and other enterprise software leaders.

strategic_fit.thesis_alignment: Accel is heavily focused on AI with their $650M AI-focused fund (raised May 2024) for Europe and Israel. They have invested in Anthropic, H Company, and numerous AI-native applications.
recent_activity: Raised $650M AI-focused fund (May 2024)
Invested in Anthropic, H Company, Nebius
Accel Atoms X AI cohort program (2025)
Published 2025 Globalscape AI report
strategic_fit.warm_path_potential: High - Philippe's cloud/enterprise focus and Accel's AI-first mandate make this a strong strategic fit

key_talking_points: Reference Accel's AI-first mandate and Globalscape report

partner.name: Jerry Chen
partner.title: General Partner
partner.investment_thesis: Former VMware VP of Cloud; searches for ambitious founders redefining enterprise software. Focus on AI-first companies and next-generation enterprise infrastructure.
partner.bio_excerpt: Jerry searches for ambitious founders who are redefining enterprise software. He was formerly VP of Cloud and Application Services at VMware.

strategic_fit.thesis_alignment: Greylock raised a $1B Fund 17 focused on 'AI-first companies' across Cyber, Infra, SaaS, and Consumer. They are early-stage specialists with a strong enterprise AI portfolio including Cresta.
recent_activity: Raised $1B Fund 17 for AI-first companies (2024)
Invested in Resolve AI ($35M seed, October 2024)
Strong enterprise AI portfolio: Cresta, Abnormal Security
Chronosphere acquisition by Palo Alto Networks ($3.35B, Nov 2025)
strategic_fit.warm_path_potential: Very High - Jerry's enterprise infrastructure background and Greylock's AI-first mandate create ideal conditions for enterprise AI agent investment

outreach_context.recommended_angle: AI agents for enterprise workflows - pre-seed/seed stage
key_talking_points: Connect to Cresta and enterprise AI portfolio
Focus on infrastructure and platform approach

partner.name: Steve Harrick
partner.title: General Partner
partner.investment_thesis: Joined IVP in 2001; over 25 years of venture capital experience. Focuses on later-stage technology companies with exceptional growth potential in enterprise software.
partner.bio_excerpt: Steve Harrick joined IVP as a General Partner in October 2001 and has over 25 years of experience in venture capital. He focuses on investing in later-stage technology companies with exceptional growth potential.

strategic_fit.thesis_alignment: IVP published the 'Enterprise AI 55' report in 2024, highlighting their focus on AI-native enterprise companies. They have an 'intense focus on artificial intelligence pioneers' and recently raised a $1.6B 18th fund.
recent_activity: Published IVP Enterprise AI 55 report (2024)
Raised $1.6B 18th fund (March 2024)
Invested in Perplexity, Superhuman, Brex AI initiatives
Focus on AI pioneers in enterprise infrastructure
strategic_fit.warm_path_potential: High - IVP's Enterprise AI 55 demonstrates commitment to the space; growth-stage focus aligns with scaling enterprise AI deployment

key_talking_points: Highlight enterprise traction and scaling metrics
